authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Russian River main stem below East Branch Russian River confluence
reaches.fromDesc: Confluence of the East Branch Russian River, near Calpella (Mendocino Co.)
reaches.toDesc: Mouth at the Pacific Ocean, Jenner (Sonoma Co.)

reaches.name: Russian River main stem above East Branch confluence and all River tributaries
reaches.fromDesc: Headwaters near Redwood Valley / Lake Mendocino (Mendocino Co.)
reaches.toDesc: Confluence of the East Branch Russian River, near Calpella (Mendocino Co.)

reaches.name: Russian River within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam
reaches.fromDesc: 125 feet upstream of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam (Healdsburg Veterans Memorial Beach)
reaches.toDesc: 125 feet downstream of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam

reaches.name: Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area
reaches.fromDesc: Mouth of the Russian River estuary, below mean high tide line

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(79) — Russian River and tributaries (Sonoma and Mendocino Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (79) Russian River and tributaries (Sonoma and Mendocino Cos.). Also see Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions. (A) Russian River main stem below the confluence of the East Branch Russian River. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) Russian River main stem above the confluence of the East Branch and all River tributaries. (See Laguna de Santa Rosa (subsection (b)(52)) and Santa Rosa Creek (subsection (b)(98)) for non-salmonids only.) Closed to all fishing all year. (C) Russian River within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam. Closed to all fishing all year. (D) Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area. See Title 14, Section 632(b)(37). Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: 14 CCR §8.00(b) — Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County Coastal Streams: Special Low Flow Conditions Closures
sources.quotedText: Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County coastal streams: Stream Closures: Special Low Flow Conditions.

sources.title: 14 CCR §632(b)(37) — Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area
sources.quotedText: (37) Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area. (A) This area includes the waters below the mean high tide line eastward of the mouth of the Russian River estuary defined as a line connecting the following two points: 38° 27.160' N. lat. 123° 07.910' W. long.; 38° 27.010' N. lat. 123° 07.740' W. long. and westward of the Highway 1 Bridge. (B) Waterfowl may be taken in accordance with the general waterfowl regulations (Sections 502, 550, 551, and 552). (C) Take of all living, geological, or cultural marine resources is prohibited.

sources.title: CDFW — Russian River State Marine Conservation Area / State Marine Recreational Management Area
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

groups.humanSummary: Russian River main stem below the East Branch Russian River confluence: open all year, artificial lures with barbless hooks only Apr. 1–Oct. 31, then barbless hooks only Nov. 1–Mar. 31, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession).
groups.verbatimText: (A) Russian River main stem below the confluence of the East Branch Russian River. All year. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Russian River main stem above the East Branch confluence, and all River tributaries: closed to all fishing all year (Laguna de Santa Rosa and Santa Rosa Creek separately regulated for non-salmonids only).
groups.verbatimText: (B) Russian River main stem above the confluence of the East Branch and all River tributaries. (See Laguna de Santa Rosa (subsection (b)(52)) and Santa Rosa Creek (subsection (b)(98)) for non-salmonids only.) Closed to all fishing all year.

groups.humanSummary: Russian River within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam: closed to all fishing all year.
groups.verbatimText: (C) Russian River within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam. Closed to all fishing all year.

groups.humanSummary: Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area (Title 14 §632(b)(37)): closed to all fishing all year.
groups.verbatimText: (D) Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area. See Title 14, Section 632(b)(37). Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.description: This water is also subject to 14 CCR §8.00(b), the Mendocino/Sonoma/Marin County coastal streams special low-flow closure provision, under which fishing may be closed when streamflow at a designated gauge falls below a prescribed threshold.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to Section 8.00(b) low-flow stream closures.
Open all year, main stem below the East Branch Russian River confluence.
Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used April 1 through October 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from Apr. 1 through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Applies November 1 through March 31; bait is allowed on barbless hooks.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used November 1 through March 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, main stem below the East Branch confluence.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Russian River main stem above the East Branch confluence and all River tributaries are closed to all fishing all year, except Laguna de Santa Rosa and Santa Rosa Creek, which are separately regulated (non-salmonids only) under subsections (b)(52) and (b)(98) respectively.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, main stem above the East Branch confluence and all River tributaries (protects listed steelhead and salmon spawning/rearing habitat).
regulations.verbatimText: Russian River main stem above the confluence of the East Branch and all River tributaries. (See Laguna de Santa Rosa (subsection (b)(52)) and Santa Rosa Creek (subsection (b)(98)) for non-salmonids only.) Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam (seasonal summer dam at Healdsburg Veterans Memorial Beach).

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam.
regulations.verbatimText: Russian River within 250 feet of the Healdsburg Memorial Dam. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area, a marine protected area at the river mouth/estuary near Jenner; closed to all fishing all year (see Title 14 §632(b)(37) for the precise MPA boundary).

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year within the Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area.
regulations.verbatimText: Russian River State Marine Recreational Management Area. See Title 14, Section 632(b)(37). Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
